exactFailureMechanism.primary: Destructive delete-then-conditional-insert in runExtraction: DELETE always runs after successful extract-invoice response, but INSERT only runs when items.length > 0. Empty extraction (HTTP 200, items:[]) wipes existing rows with no replacement.
exactFailureMechanism.secondary: Non-atomic replace allows concurrent runExtraction calls to interleave DELETE/INSERT, producing duplicate row batches (Emporio 8+8=16).
exactFailureMechanism.tertiary: INSERT failure after DELETE leaves invoice_items empty; catch swallows error and returns null.
codePath: reExtract metadata update if result truthy → invoices.tsx:2091-2133

preferredFix.name: Replace-only-when-ready guard + in-flight mutex
preferredFix.summary: Smallest safe frontend change in runExtraction: never DELETE unless normalized replacement rows exist; reject concurrent extractions for the same invoiceId via synchronous ref lock; surface empty/partial persistence skip to user.

steps.action: Add extractionInFlightRef: Record<string, boolean> (or Set) at component level
steps.detail: At runExtraction entry (before any await): if ref[invoiceId] is true, return null immediately. Set ref[invoiceId]=true synchronously; clear in finally alongside setExtracting.
Move normalizeInvoiceItemFields + shouldRejectInvoiceIngredientRow filter to immediately after items array is parsed, before any DELETE.

If normalizedItems.length === 0 after successful extract response: skip DELETE and INSERT entirely; return { ..., itemsCount: 0, persistenceSkipped: true, reason: 'no_accepted_rows' }. Existing DB rows preserved.
const { error: deleteError } = await supabase.from('invoice_items').delete()...; if (deleteError) throw deleteError;

When persistenceSkipped or result.itemsCount===0 on re-read of invoice with prior items: show toast 'Re-read returned no line items — existing rows kept.' Do not call loadItems refresh that implies success.

steps.action: Optional threshold warning for partial replace
steps.detail: If normalizedItems.length < prior item count by >50%, show confirm-style warning before delete on NEXT run — defer to phase 2; not required for smallest safe fix.

preferredFix.whyNotBackendFirst: extract-invoice does not persist items; moving replace logic to edge function or RPC would be larger scope (auth, user_id, cost sync). Frontend guard fixes observed bugs with ~15-25 LOC.
preferredFix.whyNotSchemaChange: Unique index on (invoice_id, name) would not prevent empty wipe or 8+8 duplicates with GPT-renamed rows; does not address delete-before-insert ordering.

highConfidence: Aviludo trace documents exact DELETE→skip INSERT sequence
Emporio duplicate trace documents 9ms dual-insert batches
Binder-style reverse simulation not needed — code path is explicit
uncertainty: 8% — edge cases if product intentionally wants clear-on-empty (none found)
Insert failure recovery still returns null without row restore (mitigated by delete guard + delete error check, not full transaction)

changes: uploadOne first-extract empty handling — optional toast only

items.risk: First upload with empty extraction leaves 0 items
items.note: Same as today; no prior rows to preserve

items.risk: User cannot force-clear bad rows via empty re-read
items.mitigation: Add explicit 'Clear line items' admin action separately if needed

items.risk: Concurrent re-read from two tabs still races without ref
items.mitigation: Ref mutex is per-browser-session; cross-tab needs DB advisory lock (phase 2)

items.risk: Partial extraction (3 of 9 rows) still replaces all
items.note: Existing behavior; out of scope for smallest fix

evidence: Aviludo April c2f52357: 9 items wiped to 0 on empty re-read
Emporio Italia 17aa3591: 8 items became 16 on concurrent re-read
Any invoice re-read during extract-invoice 0-item flake is vulnerable
Insert failure after delete would silently zero items
existingCorruptionToday.detection: Query invoice_items count vs expected; check for duplicate normalized names per invoice_id; compare created_at clusters

phase2Optional: Supabase RPC: replace_invoice_items(invoice_id, rows[]) in single transaction
Partial-replace confirmation when new count << prior count
Cross-tab lock via extraction_generation column on invoices
Backend validation: reject 0-item replace for invoices with existing items
